'''Sobel Zeelesi''' was a [[Color/Legends|red]]-spined [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the last [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A criminal with ties to [[Arms dealer/Legends|arms dealers]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], by [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] Zeelesi had been linked to a series of attacks that destroyed or severely damaged key facilities of the [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Force]]. For these crimes, a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was placed on his head by the RSF offering 1,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for his capture alive and 500 credits [[Death/Legends|dead]].<ref name="BH" />

When the bounty was placed, Zeelesi and his old partner [[Burbakker Teep]] were working with the smugglers of [[Muttani]] crime boss [[Meeko Ghintee]]'s syndicate. The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] ran into Zeelesi on [[Outland Transit Station|Outland Station]] in the changing rooms of the station's pit training yard. Zeelesi shot at Fett with his [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] while the three [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorreans]] guarding him rushed the Mandalorian. Having been informed of Zeelesi's bounty by the [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] owner of the station, [[Rozatta]], Jango captured him alive for 1,000 credits.<ref name="BH" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', Sobel is the third or fourth of five possible secondary bounties that a player can secure in the pit fighters' training area section of Chapter One. Players have the option of taking him alive or killing him; however, as Sobel was worth 1,000 credits alive and only 500 dead, this article assumes Fett took him alive for the higher reward.<ref name="BH" />
